comment.body:
Yo, since the market’s been rangebound, Alma’s been saying you’ve got way more edge on simple short‐vol trades than trying to pick a direction on the index. Intraday whipsaws and mean reversions are all over the place. But she’s spotting the odds shifting..looks like a short‐lived pullback is getting primed..so she’s tweaking her exposure, moving from delta-neutral (or a smidge long) into a short-delta stance.
That said, the market’s totally riding on headlines right now, so we should chill ’til the FOMC, V-Day, and the key data drops before we go full-blown net short or long on spot. The Fed and the powers-that-be wanna keep the tape propped up for now and the market’s eating up whatever they say. We all know this pump’s a ruse, so let’s see what they really want before we make any big moves

comment.body:
Most ppl fall for the mistake to try to trade everything like if somebody would put a gun to your head to do. But if I would ask, why you made this or that decision, you could not answer properly.
This is a mental trap. I tell you what I mean by that.
You have $800 on your account. You go trading and you lose $300
Next day you win again $500
Next day you lose $200, but you get a successful 'revenge trading' and win $400
You get overconfident and lose $700 on the next day. 
And finally on the last day you can gain $300.

Now listen...
 -300 + 500 - 200 + 400 - 700 + 300 = 0

Think about it...

comment.body: She laid it out crystal in her weekly post that she’s expecting those supportive flows to keep the tape propped up until that mid-summer liquidity crisis hits. Overall, she’s got better edge on short-delta plays in non-linear derivatives (e.g. options) simply cuz puts are being sold and squeezed, so they’re flat-out underpriced right now. She’s not penciling in a multi-hundred-point crash. Read her posts carefully
